assumptions: No Docusign account, developer sandbox, app, integration key, secret, RSA key, user GUID, redirect URI, consent, Quickstart ZIP, Node.js environment, signer identity, or envelope is assumed.
A supported computer, browser, terminal, npm environment, email address, and a signer name and email are available.

No account form was submitted, no verification or login was completed, and no app, integration key, secret, RSA key, consent, Quickstart, package installation, local server, or signing request was created during research.

developer_goal: Start without a Docusign account, create the free developer sandbox account, generate the main eSignature REST API Node.js Quickstart, run its preconfigured project locally, authenticate through its documented first-run Authorization Code Grant route, and open the embedded-signing example.

first_success.outcome: The intended first success is the preconfigured main Node.js Quickstart running locally, authenticating with Authorization Code Grant, and opening its embedded-signing code example.
first_success.observable_signal: The local Quickstart opens the embedded-signing example after authentication, which the official overview identifies as its first-run demonstration of basic Docusign functionality; the docs do not identify a later completed-envelope response as part of this default terminal.

route_selection.surface: Docusign eSignature REST API main Node.js Quickstart first run
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Docusign eSignature REST API main Node.js Quickstart first run
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Authorization Code Grant embedded signing Quickstart
candidates.reason: This smaller specialized project includes one embedded-signing workflow, while the overview labels Multiple code examples as the main Quickstart and documents its first-run behavior.

candidates.name: JWT Grant remote signing Quickstart
candidates.reason: This smaller specialized project includes one remote-signing workflow and uses JWT, while the main project's documented first run uses Authorization Code Grant and opens embedded signing.

candidates.name: Manually configured Node.js code launcher
candidates.reason: The launcher exposes the same broad code examples but requires manual account configuration. The main Quickstart ZIP is preconfigured and has a documented first-run example.

required_path.action: Complete DocuSign account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new DocuSign account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected DocuSign candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The local Quickstart opens the embedded-signing example after authentication, which the official overview identifies as its first-run demonstration of basic Docusign functionality; the docs do not identify a later completed-envelope response as part of this default terminal.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Create a free Docusign developer account for the sandbox environment and be its owner or account admin, because Quickstart creates a new app and integration key.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2', 'S3', 'S4']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'configuration', 'requirement': 'Name the app, select Node.js, and choose the main Multiple code examples, Authorization Code Grant and JWT Grant project; Quickstart creates and preconfigures its app, integration key, account settings, and authentication code.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S4']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'credential', 'requirement': "For a manually configured launcher instead of the selected Quickstart, provide an integration key and secret for Authorization Code Grant, or an integration key, RSA key pair, and impersonated user's User ID GUID for JWT Grant.", 'required': False, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

external_gates.description: The developer-account form requires personal identity, company, country or region, and build-intent fields.
Get Started accepts Docusign's Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy.

Verification, sandbox provisioning, and authenticated Quickstart access are hidden after form submission, and Quickstart requires the account owner or admin because it creates an app and integration key.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: On the first run of the main Quickstart project, authenticate with Authorization Code Grant and open the embedded-signing example.

excluded.item: Completing the embedded signature, confirming an envelope response, remote signing, polling envelope status, downloading completed documents, adding templates or recipients, production go-live review,
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does DocuSign show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
